status: P1 EVIDENCE CLOSURE WAVE 3 / IDENTITY OR PRODUCT-EQUIVALENCE BLOCK ACTIVE / NOT PLATINUM CERTIFIED
mechanism_summary: Clinical coadministration programmes study amylin/calcitonin-family and GLP-1 signalling. They do not validate the catalogue mixed vial.
evidence_maturity: Combination clinical-development evidence exists, but supplier-product identity, formulation, stability and equivalence are not established.
regulatory_headline: CLINICAL-DEVELOPMENT COMBINATION CONTEXT EXISTS / CATALOGUE VIAL HAS NO AUTHORISED-PRODUCT EQUIVALENCE

catalogue_supplier_skus.specification: Cagrilintide 5 mg + Semaglutide 5 mg; 10 mg x 10 vials

closure_state: PARTIAL CLINICAL-PROGRAMME CLOSURE — PHASE 3 CAGRISEMA EVIDENCE AND CURRENT US REGULATORY REVIEW IDENTIFIED; CS10 COMPONENT, FORMULATION AND PRODUCT EQUIVALENCE OPEN
authoritative_product_boundary: CagriSema clinical evidence concerns sponsor-controlled cagrilintide and semaglutide at defined doses, formulations and manufacturing standards. Novo Nordisk reported in June 2026 that a US NDA filed in December 2025 remained under review, with a decision expected in Q4 2026. The supplier CS10 co-lyophilised 5 mg/5 mg vial is not CagriSema clinical material and does not inherit trial or future approval status.
critical_closure_controls: CS10 is not CagriSema clinical material
Phase 3 efficacy does not establish supplier-vial efficacy
Current NDA review is not FDA approval
Component labels cannot be added together as a dosing protocol
No weight-loss, diabetes, dosing, injection or reconstitution protocol
